title: Shift Closing and Handover
description: Full closing cycle: from last guest to handing off the shift to the next manager.

steps.title: Final hour: Last Call
steps.content: 60 minutes before close — warn the team: quick items only. 30 minutes before — stop-list on the kitchen (no complex dishes). 15 minutes before — last call to guests ('We're closing in 15 minutes, anything else?'). Never rush guests out harshly.
steps.key_takeaway: 60 → 30 → 15 minutes. Three warning waves, not one abrupt 'we're closed'.

steps.title: Kitchen closing
steps.content: Chef: count inventory, photograph the fridge, dispose of expired items with a write-off form. Sanitize all surfaces, drain fryer oil, turn off the hood 20 minutes after the last fry. Log fridge temperatures.
steps.key_takeaway: Write-offs go on a form, not silently into the trash. That's company money.

steps.title: Dining room cleanup
steps.content: Tables: align, sanitize, set flatware to standard. Floor: sweep → wet mop → dry wipe. Restrooms: separate equipment set, checklist on the door. Windows and storefront — weekly (Fridays). Take trash to the dumpster, tie bags.
steps.key_takeaway: Restroom equipment is SEPARATE. Mixing = health inspection fine.

steps.title: Cash drop
steps.content: Count cash twice (you + witness from staff). Reconcile with the Z-report. Over $500 — drop to safe immediately. Fill the cash log: date, amount, signature. Discrepancy > $2 — incident memo, witness signature, cash drawer photo.
steps.key_takeaway: Two counters, full documentation. Hidden discrepancies = criminal liability.

steps.title: Shift report
steps.content: Fill the shift form: revenue, check count, average ticket, guest count, conversion, top-3 dishes, complaints/compliments, incidents (fights, fire, damage). Photo of the log. Submit to the manager chat by 11:59 PM.
steps.key_takeaway: No report by midnight = shift not closed. Accounting won't accept it.

steps.title: Safety walkthrough
steps.content: Walk through: all windows closed, all valves (water, gas) shut, kitchen equipment off except fridges, dining lights off, alarm armed. Cameras: confirm they're recording (indicator on). Lock the door with two turns of the key.
steps.key_takeaway: Gas → water → electric. Three shut valves = quiet night.

steps.title: Handover
steps.content: If handing to next manager: walk the floor and kitchen together, show status, hand over keys and cash, both sign the handover log. Document problems in writing (not 'you'll see tomorrow'). No signature = no handover.
steps.key_takeaway: Signed log = your protection. No signature means no handover.

quiz.question: How many minutes before closing is last call announced?
quiz.explanation: 15 minutes is optimal: guests have time to pay without feeling rushed.

quiz.question: What do you do with expired products?
quiz.explanation: A write-off form is mandatory for accounting, protecting the business.

quiz.question: At what cash discrepancy is an incident memo required?
quiz.explanation: Over $2 = mandatory documentation. Below = standard cash log only.

quiz.question: By when must the shift report be submitted?
quiz.explanation: By midnight — or accounting won't close the day.

quiz.question: What must be shut down before leaving?
options: Gas, water, windows closed, alarm on
quiz.explanation: Gas-water-windows-alarm is the basic safety checklist.

manager_checklist: Team was warned 60/30/15 minutes before closing
Dining room cleaned to standard (separate restroom equipment)
Cash counted by two people, amount documented
Handover log signed by both parties

kpi.how_to_measure: Log timestamps: last order vs departure
